name: Daisy
fact: Daisies close their petals at night and reopen at dawn — the name comes from 'day's eye'.

name: Sunflower
fact: Young sunflowers track the sun across the sky each day, a behavior called heliotropism.

name: Clover
fact: Only about 1 in 5,000 clovers has four leaves — most have three.

name: Tulip
fact: In 1630s Holland, a single tulip bulb could cost more than a house — peak 'Tulip Mania'.

name: Lavender
fact: Lavender belongs to the mint family and bees absolutely love it.

name: Mushroom
fact: Mushrooms aren't plants at all — they're more closely related to animals than to flowers!

name: Bluebell
fact: Bluebell woods can take over a century to establish — they're a sign of ancient forest.

name: Lotus
fact: Lotus seeds can germinate after 1,000+ years of dormancy — they're nature's time capsules.

name: Moonflower
fact: Moonflowers bloom only at night, unfurling in minutes to attract moths under the stars.

name: Crystal Rose
fact: Legend says a Crystal Rose only blooms when watched by someone who truly believes.

name: Dandelion
fact: Every part of a dandelion is edible — leaves, flowers, and even the roots.

name: Poppy
fact: A single poppy plant can produce up to 60,000 tiny seeds in one season.

name: Cactus
fact: A saguaro cactus can live over 150 years and store hundreds of gallons of water in its trunk.

name: Maple Sapling
fact: It takes about 40 gallons of maple sap to make just one gallon of maple syrup.

name: Fern
fact: Ferns predate dinosaurs by 200 million years and reproduce via spores, not seeds.

name: Hyacinth
fact: Hyacinths are named after a Greek mythological figure beloved by the god Apollo.

name: Cherry Blossom
fact: Japan tracks cherry blossom forecasts on the news — full bloom typically lasts only a week.

name: Pumpkin Vine
fact: The world record pumpkin weighed over 2,700 pounds — heavier than a small car.

name: Bonsai
fact: The oldest known bonsai is over 1,000 years old and still being cared for in Japan.

name: Phoenix Lily
fact: Phoenix Lilies are said to sprout brightest in soil scorched by wildfire — beauty from ash.

name: Frostflower
fact: Real frostflowers form when sap freezes and bursts through plant stems on cold mornings.

name: Galaxy Rose
fact: Each Galaxy Rose petal is said to hold a tiny constellation — no two are ever the same.
